symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden high fever (39.6) with severe generalised myalgia

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: High fever with prominent myalgia is a constitutional feature of a toxin-mediated systemic illness.
Fever is a core sepsis feature.

symptoms.symptom_name: Diffuse macular erythroderma resembling sunburn over trunk and limbs

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Diffuse macular erythroderma is a defining feature of TSS.
Meningococcal rash is characteristically non-blanching petechial/purpuric, not a diffuse blanching erythema.
Anaphylactic skin change is typically raised itchy urticaria/wheals, not a flat macular sunburn-like rash.

symptoms.symptom_name: Near-syncope on sitting/standing with new confusion and drowsiness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Postural near-syncope plus altered mentation signals distributive shock with cerebral hypoperfusion — the immediate threat to life.
Hypotension and altered mental status are hallmark markers of shock.

symptoms.symptom_name: Conjunctival and oropharyngeal hyperaemia (red eyes and mouth)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mucous-membrane hyperaemia ('strawberry tongue', conjunctival injection) is a recognised diagnostic criterion for TSS.
A retained high-absorbency tampon during menses is the classic menstrual risk factor and identifies the removable toxin source.

symptoms.symptom_name: Profuse watery diarrhoea and repeated vomiting

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Early profuse vomiting and watery diarrhoea are characteristic GI features of TSS and worsen the volume depletion.
GI losses alone are non-specific — the fever, rash and shock override a benign gastro interpretation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Measured hypotension BP 80/45 with tachycardia HR 135

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective distributive shock — a clinician-measured vital sealed on the answer-key side; the AI infers circulatory compromise from reported near-syncope and confusion, not a measured number.

symptoms.symptom_name: Altered conscious level (GCS 13) without focal neurology

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Formal conscious-level scoring is a clinician assessment; the reported confusion is its telehealth proxy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Isolation of Staphylococcus aureus / Streptococcus pyogenes from a focal site

investigation_details.investigation_name: Culture of vaginal swab / focal site plus blood cultures
investigation_details.expected_result: Growth of toxigenic S. aureus (or S. pyogenes for the streptococcal form)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Source organism and toxin type not confirmed; retrospective, hospital-only test.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Microbiological isolation supports the toxin-producing source; never available in real time via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Multi-organ dysfunction on bloods (elevated creatinine and transaminases)

investigation_details.investigation_name: Renal and liver function panel, lactate, full blood count
investigation_details.expected_result: Raised creatinine and transaminases indicating renal and hepatic involvement
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Degree of organ failure unquantified; hospital-only investigation.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Multisystem (>=3 organ) involvement is a diagnostic criterion; requires bloods unavailable in telehealth.
Characteristic but LATE (1-2 weeks) feature — absent acutely, so its absence now does not argue against the diagnosis.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Diffuse sunburn-like erythroderma + profound hypotension + high fever + mucosal hyperaemia is the catastrophic toxic-shock constellation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Immediate 000 ambulance transfer to ED/ICU (not an office investigation)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Definitive assessment, bloods, cultures and resuscitation must occur in hospital — the telehealth-correct 'investigation' is emergency transfer.
Do not delay the 000 call to hunt for a home BP reading; the clinical picture already mandates emergency escalation and a low reading adds nothing but delay.
